ns1:revision.ns1:text:
OpenSFS hosted a Lustre Developer Day in Houston, TX on May 14th, 2019. The meeting was held the day before [[Lustre User Group 2019]].
The agenda was as follows:
* [[Media:LUG2019-Developer_Day-OST_Pool_Quotas-Cray.pdf|OST Pool Quotas]] (Cray) * [[Media:LUG2019-Developer_Day-ls_Long_Term_Storage-Rutman.pdf|Efficient 'ls -l', Lustre for long-term storage]] (Nathan Rutman) * [[Media:LUG2019-Developer_Day-Lustre_Upstreaming-Brown.pdf|Upstreaming: buy-in, process, pain points]] (Neil Brown) * [[Media:LUG2019-Developer_Day-Leveraging_Newer_Kernels-Simmons.pdf|Leveraging options in newer Linux kernels]] (James Simmons) * OpenSFS Requirement Gathering Discussion (Ken Rawlings) * LNet Network Namespace (Aurélien Degremont) * [[Media:LUG2019-Developer_Day-ldiskfs_Status_Foreign_Layouts-Dilger.pdf|Ldiskfs Status, Foreign Layouts]] (Andreas Dilger) * [[Media:LUG2019-Developer_Day-Security_Encryption-Buisson.pdf|Security and Encryption]] (Sebastien Buisson) * [[Media:LUG2019-Developer_Day-Fujitsu_Development_Plans-Sumimoto.pdf|Fujitsu Development Plans]] (Shinji Sumimoto)
[[Category: Events]] [[Category: LUG]]
